mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in FEDFUNDS lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, so lower policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is confirmed when rate cuts are followed by above-baseline SPX gains and refuted when cuts coincide with flat or negative 20-day returns.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-month window but SPX 20-day forward returns are not positive more often than not, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC is below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in CPIAUCSL relative to the prior 3 months signals inflation re-acceleration that raises real-rate pressure and weakens equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is falsified if inflation jumps do not precede weaker SPX performance.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A falling UNRATE indicates a tightening labor market and stronger household income support, which lifts equity demand and forward SPX returns over the next 60 trading days. The claim fails when unemployment declines but equity returns do not improve within one quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns after a 0.3pp 3-month UNRATE decline are not statistically higher than the unconditional distribution, or if the sign accuracy falls below 55% in triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: A broad rise in RSAFS captures stronger nominal spending and revenue growth, which improves earnings expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism is invalid if retail sales gains do not coincide with equity strength.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month moving average rises above its 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if periods where RSAFS trend is above its 12-month average do not show higher 20-day SPX returns than baseline, or if rolling Rank IC stays below 0.03 for 2 consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: An upturn in INDPRO signals improving industrial output and profit expectations, which should lead to positive SPX returns over the next 5 trading days. The relation is falsified if industrial production surges without a near-immediate equity response.
failure_condition: Reject if weekly-like 5-day SPX returns are not positive on average after positive INDPRO momentum observations, or if the sign hit rate is below 52% over the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising HOUST and rising PAYEMS indicates synchronized housing demand and labor income momentum, which expands cyclical growth expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. Each series alone is insufficient; the signal exists only when both improve together.
failure_condition: Reject if joint-positive HOUST and PAYEMS changes do not outperform single-series or unconditional cases in 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is insignificant in rolling walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation is still rising, the market should price tighter financial conditions and lower near-term equity returns over 20 trading days. The claim is confirmed only if high rates combined with accelerating inflation precede negative SPX performance.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rising
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not yield below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if outcomes are indistinguishable from cases where only one of the two proxies is adverse.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained acceleration in GDPC1 growth identifies a broad real-demand upswing that should support S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is strongest after confirmed positive GDP revisions or multi-quarter growth acceleration and fails if stronger GDP growth is not followed by higher forward equity returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 2-quarter annualized growth turns positive and rises versus prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 growth acceleration does not lead to higher 60-day SPX returns than the unconditional mean, or if the sign accuracy remains below 55% in growth-acceleration windows.
